image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Did you know Earth is closest to the Sun in January?
Context visual: So why are we freezing in winter?
Escalation visual: It's all about axial tilt—not distance. Earth's axis is tilted at 23.5 degrees.
Evidence visual: This tilt causes different parts of Earth to receive varying sunlight throughout the year.
Payoff visual: In summer, the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the Sun, heating things up!
Pattern Interrupt visual: So, the next time you enjoy the sun, remember—it’s not about how close we are.
Cta visual: Follow for more mind-blowing science facts!
